expectations.text: Explains SSL errors are often transient, not necessarily fatal
expectations.evidence: "SSL errors with `colab exec` are usually transient network issues. The session and your background process (PID 903) may be perfectly fine."

expectations.text: Shows how to verify session with colab sessions / colab status
expectations.evidence: "Run this before anything else: ```bash colab sessions && colab status ```"

expectations.text: Mentions the background PID (903) may still be alive despite SSL errors
expectations.evidence: "The session and your background process (PID 903) may be perfectly fine." and "```bash echo 'import os, signal; print(os.kill(903, 0) is None)' | colab exec --timeout 10 ```"

expectations.text: Mentions ~2-4 hour session lifetime and silent termination
expectations.evidence: "it was pruned (free Colab sessions last ~2-4 hours)" and "free tier is ~2-4 hours total runtime"

expectations.text: Shows recovery: new session → re-upload → resume from checkpoints
expectations.evidence:
"# Create a new session
colab new --gpu T4 --session my-training-v2

# Re-upload your code and your last checkpoint
colab upload train.py train.py
colab upload checkpoints/best.pt checkpoints/best.pt

# Re-launch with --resume flag"

expectations.text: Advises downloading checkpoints before session dies: colab download checkpoints/
expectations.evidence: The output advises downloading checkpoints generally ("Download checkpoints periodically during training") but does not show the specific command `colab download checkpoints/`.
